title: The Jersey Stack — Ep 1: The Transistor (DRAFT v8 — 720p preview)
description:
🎬 DRAFT v8 (720p preview cut) — for review only, not for public sharing. Full 1080p master available locally; 720p uploaded for fast review.

New Jersey invented the transistor. A 20-mile stretch of NJ holds the birthplace of the thing that runs every device in your pocket, and a monument to itself just outside the Severance set. Episode 1 of The Jersey Stack follows the story from Bell Labs Murray Hill in 1947 through the Traitorous Eight walkout in 1957 — and ends back at Fort Monmouth, where Netflix is rebuilding the ground that built the hardware.

What changed v7 → v8:
• Scene 7 — new opening AI image visualizing the 'solid-state' bet: a glowing vacuum tube vs. a cold germanium crystal slab on the same bench (no moving parts, no glowing filaments)
• Scene 13 — new AI imagery for Bardeen's surface-state insight (electrons trapped on the germanium surface) and the November–December 1947 push at Lab 1E-455 in Murray Hill
• Scene 22 — archival 1946 photo of the Project Diana antenna at Camp Evans (Wikimedia, PD-USGov) anchors the 'same soil, different century' beat alongside the modern InfoAge creator photos
• Scene 24 — new AI image of a 1956 federal courtroom for the AT&T consent decree beat

What changed v6 → v7 (still in this cut):
• Scene 25 + 26 merged into a single Diaspora beat (~70s) with the new thesis ending: 'Silicon Valley came from New Jersey. California scaled it. But Jersey built it.'
• Scene 29 cut to a tight closing beat — 'That is not a coincidence. That's continuity.'

What changed v5 → v6 (still in this cut):
• Pacing fix — every multi-image scene now stretches each panel to fill its full narration duration. v5's xfade math was eating 0.5s per transition; all scenes now land within ~30ms of their narration budget.

Known issues still in this cut:
• Scene 27 motion-graphic counter (1 → 92,000,000,000) still not rendered — static images stand in
• Some AI-generated text (S20 newspaper, S26 logo cascade) may have rendering artifacts

Chapters:
0:00 Drone Reveal & Severance Hook
0:38 Bell Labs Murray Hill
2:32 The Vacuum Tube Problem
3:42 The Trio (Bardeen, Brattain, Shockley)
5:32 December 23, 1947 — The Demo
7:08 Shockley Finds Out — the Junction Transistor
8:46 The June 1948 Press Conference & Fort Monmouth
10:45 1956 — Nobel & the Consent Decree
11:45 Shockley Goes West & the Diaspora
12:53 The Count-Up — 1 to 92 Billion
13:28 Back to the Drone — Continuity
13:32 Next Episode — The Switch

---
Part of The Jersey Stack — a five-episode series on how New Jersey invented the modern computer.
